evals.prompt: Run a security audit on our Node.js backend codebase before we go live.
evals.expected_output: Execute 7-step audit: (1) Scan for hardcoded secrets (grep for API_KEY, PASSWORD, SECRET). (2) Check logs for PII (grep for password/token in console.log). (3) Map injection surfaces (raw string concat in queries). (4) Measure auth coverage (total routes vs guarded routes). (5) Run npm audit --audit-level=high. (6) Audit infrastructure. (7) Check for SSRF/path traversal/BOLA. Report with scoring.

evals.prompt: We found a hardcoded database password in our Go source code. How severe is this?
evals.expected_output: P0 finding — Hardcoded Secrets: -25 deduction. Security score capped at 40/100. Immediate actions: (1) Rotate the credential NOW. (2) Use git filter-repo to purge from history. (3) Move to environment variable or secret manager. (4) Add pre-commit hook to prevent future secrets. Treat as compromised until rotation confirmed.

evals.prompt: I want to check if our API has any IDOR vulnerabilities.
evals.expected_output: Run: grep -rE 'findById\(|findOne\(' . | grep -viE 'tenant|owner|user_id'. Any findById without owner filter is a BOLA/IDOR vulnerability (P0, -15). Also check: SSRF via user-controlled URLs in axios/fetch calls, path traversal in path.join calls without normalization.

should_not_trigger: Write a CSS animation for a loading spinner
